reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 6.6% and 20-day momentum is 9.3%.
Trend quality is supported by 50% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 6.8% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.6%, downside volatility near 1.0%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.7%.
Volume confirmation is 0.5% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 5.1% versus SPY over 5 days and 4.5% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.67, confidence 0.38, and 0.1 weighted articles across 1 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.80, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 1 articles across 1 sources, 0.1 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.91, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.69.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.02).
Overall model score is 0.138 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Honeywell International Nears June 29 Split With 'All Green Lights,' Reaffirms Outlook
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct company update: confirms progress toward planned separation and reaffirms outlook. Positive tone could support sentiment, but the major event (June 29 separation) is beyond the 1–10 trading day window, so immediate material impact is limited. Operational remarks (active customers, repair work) provide modest short-term reassurance.
